prompt: This isAdult method always returns true even when age is 16. What is the bug?
choices: The method should use == instead of >= to compare age
There is a semicolon after the if condition — the if body is an empty statement; return true is unconditional
return false should be return null for ages under 18
if/else must be used; a standalone if cannot guard a return statement
explanation: The semicolon directly after 'if (age >= 18)' terminates the if statement with an empty body. The indented 'return true;' on the next line is not part of the if — it executes unconditionally every time the method is called, regardless of age. Fix: remove the semicolon so 'return true;' is inside the if block.
